fields.preview:
<system-reminder>
As you answer the user's questions, you can use the following context:
# currentDate
Today's date is 2

The following skills are available for use with the Skill tool:

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web se
You are a Claude agent, built on Anthropic's Claude Agent SDK.

You are an interactive agent that helps users with software engineering tasks.

IMPORTANT: Assist with authorized secur

instruction_fields.preview:
The following skills are available for use with the Skill tool:

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web se

items.name: deep-research
items.description: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
items.preview: - deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a

items.name: update-config
items.description: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
items.preview: - update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now o

items.name: keybindings-help
items.description: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
items.preview: - keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify

items.name: verify
items.description: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
items.preview: - verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use w

items.name: code-review
items.description: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
items.preview: - code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given ef

items.name: simplify
items.description: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
items.preview: - simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes.

items.name: fewer-permission-prompts
items.description: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
items.preview: - fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized a

items.name: loop
items.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
items.preview: - loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user want

items.name: claude-api
items.description: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
items.preview: - claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agent

items.name: run
items.description: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
items.preview: - run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app,

items.name: init
items.description: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
items.preview: - init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation

items.name: review
items.description: Review a pull request
items.preview: - review: Review a pull request

items.name: security-review
items.description: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
items.preview: - security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

items.name: mcp_1

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web se

items.name: mcp_2
items.preview:
Fetches a URL, converts the page to markdown, and answers `prompt` against it using a small fast model.

- Fails on auth

items.name: mcp_3
items.preview: Execute a workflow script that orchestrates multiple subagents deterministically. Workflows run in the background — this

items.preview: - statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)
